# Exploration: [TITLE]

**Report ID**: exploration-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS
**Date**: YYYY-MM-DD
**Status**: active | iterating | approved
**Feature Name**: [short kebab-case name for the comp folder, e.g., `settings-panel`, `nav-bar`]

## Component Description

> What this component or UI element is and what it does.

## Design Requirements

> Specific requirements driving the design. Include constraints, must-haves, and nice-to-haves.

## Experience Location

> Where in the application this component will live. What surrounds it. What context does the user have when they encounter it.

## Design System References

> Relevant colors, typography, spacing, or patterns from `.github/style/design-system.md` (if it exists).

## User Direction

> Any preferences or direction the user gave during the conversation. "I want it minimal", "think bold", "similar to X", etc.

## Questions Resolved

> Clarifying questions asked during exploration intake and how the user answered.

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| | |

## Open Questions

> Anything still needing answers.

- [ ] 

## Button Mappings

> Navigation buttons/routes that this screen must support. Klee uses these to populate `navButtons` and initial `buttonMappings` in the manifest. Each entry specifies a CSS selector matching the button element in the comp's nav bar and a human-readable label.

| Selector | Label | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| `.nav-example` | Example | |

## Related Comps

> Link to the comp set folder once Klee creates it.

- Comp folder: `.github/comps/active/[feature-name]/`
